message:
fix(auth): read last shell-config var assignment, not first

Previously both the Bash `read_configured_var_from_file` helper
(used in scripts/lib/doctor.sh and packages/onboard/onboard.sh) and
the TypeScript `readConfiguredValueFromFile` helper would return the
FIRST matching non-blank assignment in a shell config and stop
scanning. Users who kept an earlier placeholder line like
`export VERCEL_TOKEN=your_vercel_token` above a real later override
were incorrectly reported as "authenticated via VERCEL_TOKEN" when
only a placeholder was present, or vice versa — the override was
never seen because the earlier line short-circuited the loop.

All three implementations now scan every assignment line in the file
and keep the last non-blank value, matching normal shell semantics
where a later `export FOO=bar` replaces an earlier one. Two new
authChecks tests pin the behaviour in both directions: a placeholder
followed by a real token should authenticate; a real token followed
by a placeholder should not. The generated
scripts/generated/internal_checksums.sh is regenerated because
scripts/lib/doctor.sh content changed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1186,19 +1186,22 @@ read_configured_var_from_file() {
 
     local line=""
     local regex="^[[:space:]]*(export[[:space:]]+)?${var_name}[[:space:]]*=(.*)$"
+    local configured_value=""
     while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
         [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
         if [[ "$line" =~ $regex ]]; then
             local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
             value="$(strip_shell_inline_comment "$value")"
             value="$(normalize_config_value "$value")"
-            if has_nonblank_value "$value"; then
-                printf '%s\n' "$value"
-                return 0
-            fi
+            configured_value="$value"
         fi
     done < "$file_path"
 
+    if has_nonblank_value "$configured_value"; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$configured_value"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
     return 1
 }

@@ -1411,19 +1411,22 @@ read_configured_var_from_file() {
 
     local line=""
     local regex="^[[:space:]]*(export[[:space:]]+)?${var_name}[[:space:]]*=(.*)$"
+    local configured_value=""
     while IFS= read -r line || [[ -n "$line" ]]; do
         [[ "$line" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
         if [[ "$line" =~ $regex ]]; then
             local value="${BASH_REMATCH[2]}"
             value="$(strip_shell_inline_comment "$value")"
             value="$(normalize_config_value "$value")"
-            if [[ -n "${value//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
-                printf '%s\n' "$value"
-                return 0
-            fi
+            configured_value="$value"
         fi
     done < "$file_path"
 
+    if [[ -n "${configured_value//[[:space:]]/}" ]]; then
+        printf '%s\n' "$configured_value"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
     return 1
 }
